Official Player Manual · v1.0 · 2026

VR NEXUS

Silicon & Senses

A high-fidelity corporate strategy simulation. Build your startup, crush the duopoly, and dominate the VR market across 8 financial quarters.

Finance for Non-Financial Decision Makers · Simulation Module
Chapter 01

Game Overview & Objective

It is 2032. The VR market is dominated by two entrenched giants: Apex Optic, the elite gatekeeper of premium immersion, and ValueVirtua, the manufacturer of budget, low-quality headsets. The world is hungry for a third option.

You are the founder of a stealth-mode startup that has just secured a $500,000 seed round. Over 8 financial quarters, your team must design a disruptive VR headset, manage volatile cash flow, and outmaneuver both AI competitors and rival player-companies to claim market dominance.

🏆 The Winning Condition

The company with the highest Weighted Score at the end of Quarter 8 wins. The score rewards long-term financial health — not short-term revenue spikes.

Score = (0.4 × Total Equity) + (0.4 × Market Share) + (0.2 × Brand Equity)
40% — Total Equity
Shareholders' Equity + Retained Earnings
40% — Market Share
Units Sold / Total Market Units
20% — Brand Equity
Accumulated Brand Reputation Points

⏱️ Starting Conditions

ParameterStarting Value
Cash$500,000
Fixed Assets (Gross)$200,000
Component LevelLevel 1 (all four components)
Inventory0 units
Brand Equity10 pts
Debt$0
Max Production Capacity2,000 units / quarter

🔄 The Game Loop (Every Quarter)

  1. Analyze — Review your P&L, Cash Flow Statement, Balance Sheet, and Market Intelligence.
  2. Decide — Set your Price, Production Volume, Brand Spend, CapEx investment, R&D upgrades, and financing moves.
  3. Execute — Lock in decisions. The GM triggers the engine, which calculates sales, market share, and updates the ledger.
  4. Debrief — The GM leads a "War Room" session reviewing results, efficiency metrics, and competitive benchmarks.
Chapter 02

The Dashboard — Your Command Center

Accessing the simulation begins at the Welcome Screen. Enter your Company ID (provided by your Game Master) to access your private dashboard. The Game Master accesses a separate control panel via a secret password.

📋 Decisions Tab — The Quarter Decision Hub

This is where every strategic decision is made each quarter. The dashboard is split into two panels: the Decision Hub (left) and the R&D Upgrade Panel (right).

DECISIONS TAB — Quarter Decision Hub with KPI strip, pricing controls, brand spend, CapEx, financing, and R&D upgrade panel

🔹 KPI Status Strip (top bar)

Five key metrics update in real-time as you configure your decisions:

MetricColorWhat It Tells You
CashGreenCurrent liquid capital. Never let this reach $0.
Total EquityCyan / RedYour core winning metric. Green if above seed capital.
Brand EquityFuchsiaYour accumulated reputation, drives the Attractiveness Score.
Tech ScoreYellowSum of all component levels. Ranges from 4–12.
Max CapacityWhiteThe ceiling on units you can build this quarter.

🔹 Pricing & Production

Set the Unit Price ($) for your headset and the Production Volume (capped by your Max Capacity). The interface will warn you in real-time if your price exceeds the allowed maximum for your current Tech Score tier.

⚡ Pricing Rule
Your maximum allowable price is calculated as: Unit Build Cost × Tier Multiplier. Budget (Score 4–5): ×2.0, Mid-Range (6–8): ×2.5, Premium (9–12): ×3.0.

🔹 Brand Spend

Allocate your quarterly investment in brand marketing. Every $1,000 spent earns 1 Brand Equity point. Spending less than $5,000 in any quarter triggers a 5% brand decay on your existing equity balance.

🔹 Capital Expenditure (CapEx)

Invest in factories and assembly lines to grow your production ceiling. Every $1,000 of CapEx adds 10 units of quarterly capacity — but this only takes effect in the following quarter (the Lag Rule).

🔹 Financing Panel

Two financing instruments are available. Use them thoughtfully — excessive debt triggers solvency penalties.

InstrumentRateBest Used For
Revolving Credit Line12% APR / 3% per qtrShort-term cash flow gaps (inventory timing)
Bank Loan6% APR / 1.5% per qtrLong-term capital investment (CapEx, factories)
🚨 The 50% Rule — Liquidity Freeze
Total Debt cannot exceed 50% of Total Assets. If it does — or cash hits $0 — you enter a Liquidity Freeze: all Marketing and R&D spending is blocked until solvency is restored. You may still produce and sell, but you're fighting with one hand tied behind your back.

🔹 R&D — Component Upgrades Panel

On the right side of the Decisions tab, you can initiate upgrades to any of your four headset components. Each upgrade costs a one-time $150,000 R&D fee. Upgrades cannot skip levels and take effect in the next quarter.

Chapter 03

Product & Components

Every headset is an assembly of four core components. Your choices determine your Tech Score, Unit Build Cost, and ultimately your market attractiveness.

ComponentLevel 1 — BudgetLevel 2 — Mid-RangeLevel 3 — Premium
Display LCD — $120/unit OLED — $150/unit Micro-OLED — $250/unit
Optics Fresnel — $40/unit Aspheric — $80/unit Pancake — $130/unit
Tracking 3-DoF — $60/unit 6-DoF Inside-out — $120/unit 6-DoF + Eye Tracking — $180/unit
Processor Mobile Lite — $90/unit Standard SoC — $150/unit High-Performance — $220/unit

Plus a flat $120/unit Assembly Cost added to all configurations.

Tech Score

Each component contributes Tech Points equal to its level number (Level 1 = 1pt, Level 2 = 2pts, Level 3 = 3pts). Your Tech Score is the sum.

Tech Score = Display Lvl + Optics Lvl + Tracking Lvl + Processor Lvl  (Range: 4 – 12)

The Lag Rule — Nothing Is Instant

All decisions take effect the quarter after they are submitted. Plan ahead!

Decision Made in QnTakes Effect
R&D UpgradeStart of Qn+1 — new component available for production
Production VolumeUnits built in Qn are available to sell in Qn+1
CapEx (Fixed Asset purchase)New capacity operational from Qn+1
Loans / Credit DrawsCash available from Qn+1

R&D Maintenance Cost

Advanced components require ongoing engineering effort. For every component level above Level 1, you pay a recurring $15,000/quarter maintenance fee. This is a direct charge on your P&L every quarter.

💡 Example
Two components at Level 2, one at Level 3 = (2×$15k) + (1×$15k) = $45,000/quarter in R&D Maintenance, before any new upgrades.
Chapter 04

Financial Statements

The simulation generates three professional-grade financial reports after every quarter. Mastering how to read them is central to winning.

FINANCIALS TAB — Live Balance Sheet (left), Profit & Loss and Cash Flow Statement (center / right), and Historical Trend Charts (below)

A. Balance Sheet

A snapshot of your company's financial position at the end of any quarter.

LineItemNotes
A1CashLiquid capital. The lifeblood of your operations.
A2Accounts Receivable (AR)30% of previous quarter's sales revenue, not yet collected.
A4InventoryUnsold units valued at Unit Build Cost (FIFO).
A5Total Current AssetsA1 + A2 + A4
A8Net Fixed AssetsGross Fixed Assets − Accumulated Depreciation. Drives production capacity.
A9Total AssetsA5 + A8
A10Accounts Payable (AP)50% of current quarter's component costs, deferred.
A11Credit LineFlexible high-interest revolving debt.
A13Bank LoanFixed-term low-interest debt.
A18Total EquityShareholders' Equity + Retained Earnings. 40% of your winning score.
✅ Balance Sheet Check
Total Assets (A9) must always equal Total Liabilities + Total Equity (A15 + A18). Any discrepancy indicates a calculation error.

B. Profit & Loss (P&L) Statement

LineItemCalculation
B1RevenueUnits Sold × Unit Price
B2COGSUnits Sold × Unit Build Cost
B3Gross ProfitB1 − B2
B4Marketing & Brand SpendOpEx — direct cash out
B5R&D ExpensesOne-time Upgrade Fees + Recurring Maintenance ($15k × levels above 1)
B5bFixed Overhead$80,000/quarter — non-negotiable factory & ops cost
B6Depreciation12.5% of Gross Fixed Assets per quarter (capped at 80% of asset value)
B7Operating Income (EBIT)B3 − (B4+B5+B5b+B6)
B8Interest Expense(Credit Line × 3%) + (Bank Loan × 1.5%)
B9Net IncomeB7 − B8

C. Cash Flow Statement

Profit is a theory; cash is a fact. The cash flow statement reconciles these two, accounting for the timing gap between accrual accounting and actual cash movement.

LineItemFormula
C1Cash from Customers(Current Revenue × 70%) + Prior Quarter AR
C2Cash Paid for Inventory(Current Build Costs × 50%) + Prior Quarter AP
C5Net Cash from Ops (CFO)C1 − (C2 + C3 + C4)
C6Capital ExpendituresCash spent on Fixed Assets this quarter
C9Cash Flow from FinancingDebt draws − Principal repayments
C10Net Change in CashC5 − C6 + C9
Chapter 05

The Market Engine & Competitors

How Demand is Calculated

The simulation uses a Logit Demand Model. Every company's sales are governed by an Attractiveness Score (A), which captures the price-to-performance ratio amplified by brand reputation:

A = (Tech Score / Price) × Brand Equity

Your share of the total market is then proportional to your attractiveness vs. all competitors:

Company Market Share = Ayours / Σ Aall companies

Units Sold = Company Market Share × Total Market Size, capped by your available inventory. If you run out of inventory (a "stockout"), unmet demand spills over to your next most attractive competitor.

The total market starts at 10,000 units/quarter and grows at a rate set by the GM (default: 7% per quarter).

Your AI Competitors

Apex Optic PREMIUM AI

  • Tech Score: 12 (all Level 3)
  • Starting Price: $1,800/unit
  • Brand Equity: 150 pts
  • Market Share: ~35% initially
  • Never drops price below $1,500
  • Boosts brand spend if you reach Tech Score 11+

ValueVirtua BUDGET AI

  • Tech Score: 4 (all Level 1)
  • Starting Price: $500/unit
  • Brand Equity: 60 pts
  • Market Share: ~30% initially
  • Matches your price if within 10% of its own
  • Cuts price by $10/qtr if share falls below 15%
📌 Opening Market
The remaining ~35% of the 10,000-unit market is split among all player companies in Q1 based on their opening Attractiveness Scores. This is your beachhead.

Market Intelligence & Leaderboard

MARKET INTEL — Competitor price, Tech Score, Brand Equity & market conditions
LEADERBOARD — Live rankings by Weighted Score, Total Equity & Brand Equity
Chapter 06

The Game Master & War Room

Between quarters, the Game Master leads a structured War Room Briefing. This is the core educational session of the simulation — a structured debrief that connects every team's financial decisions to their market outcomes.

GM WAR ROOM — Leaderboard, configuration controls, market shock triggers, and rollback capability

War Room Agenda

  1. The Leaderboard — Rankings by Total Equity, Market Share, and Weighted Score are revealed.
  2. Competitive Benchmarking — The GM compares Fixed Asset Turnover, Inventory Days, and Gross Margin side-by-side.
  3. Market Shocks — Unexpected external events are announced (e.g., chipset shortages, OLED price drops, competitor brand scandals).
  4. Pivot Discussion — The GM spotlights a Cash Flow success story or a Debt Spiral cautionary tale.
  5. Shadow KPI Review — If a rollback occurred, the GM compares the "Old Timeline" vs. "New Timeline" outcomes for a structured "What If" discussion.

External Shock Events (Examples)

QuarterShock EventImpact
Q3Global chipset shortageLevel 3 Processor cost +20% for one quarter
Q5OLED yield breakthroughLevel 2 Display cost −15% permanently
Q6Consumer advocacy reportValueVirtua's Brand Equity halved
Chapter 07

Strategies & Pro Tips

The following tips are distilled from the rules of the simulation engine itself. Understanding the "why" behind each recommendation is the key to truly winning.

  • ⏳
    Plan One Quarter Ahead — Always The Lag Rule is the most important mechanic in the game. CapEx won't expand your capacity this quarter; a drawn loan won't give you cash right now. Every strategic move must be made one quarter before you actually need it. Reactive decision-making is a losing strategy.
  • 📣
    Never Let Brand Equity Decay Brand Equity is a multiplier in the Attractiveness Formula. A great Tech Score means nothing if your brand reputation is eroding. Maintain at least $5,000/quarter in Brand Spend at all times — even when cash is tight. Sacrificing brand for one quarter can cost you multiple quarters of market share.
  • 🏭
    Expand Cautiously — Depreciation Is Brutal Every $100,000 of Fixed Assets generates $12,500 of depreciation expense per quarter, regardless of sales. Aggressive factory expansion without matching revenue volume will crater your P&L. Scale production capacity in line with realistically achievable demand.
  • 💳
    Match Debt Maturity to Asset Maturity Use the Revolving Credit Line (12% APR) for short-term operational gaps only. Use Bank Loans (6% APR) for long-term CapEx investment. Financing a factory purchase with a revolving credit line will cause runaway interest costs that are nearly impossible to recover from.
  • 📦
    Avoid Both Stockouts and Pile-ups Excess inventory is cash tied up on a shelf — it appears on your Balance Sheet but is not earning revenue. A stockout means you lost sales to your competitors. Study the Market Intel tab before committing production volume. Produce for demand, not factory capacity.
  • 🤖
    Find the Gap Between the AI Titans Apex Optic (Tech Score 12, $1,800) and ValueVirtua (Tech Score 4, $500) deliberately leave a vacuum in the mid-range ($700–$1,200) and mid-tech (Score 6–9) tier. A Med-Range headset with strong brand investment will out-attract both AIs for a large portion of price-sensitive but quality-conscious consumers.
  • 📊
    Watch Cash Flow — Not Just Net Income A company can be profitable on the P&L while running out of cash due to inventory build-up and receivables timing (the AR/AP gap). Always check the Cash Flow Statement (C10 Net Change in Cash) before celebrating a profitable quarter.
  • 🎯
    Total Equity Is the Real Scoreboard The winning formula weights Total Equity at 40%. Strategies that generate consistent Net Income (which flows into Retained Earnings) compound over 8 quarters. A high-revenue but high-cost strategy that generates no retained earnings will always lose to a lean, efficient competitor.
Appendix

Key Terms Glossary

Attractiveness Score (A)
The primary demand driver: (Tech Score / Price) × Brand Equity. Determines your relative market share via the Logit Model.
Brand Equity
Accumulated reputation points. Gains 1 pt per $1K of brand spend; decays 5% if spend is below $5K. Capped at 200 pts.
CapEx
Capital Expenditure. Cash invested in Fixed Assets (factories). Increases production capacity by 10 units per $1K spent (next quarter).
Depreciation
Non-cash expense of 12.5% of Gross Fixed Assets per quarter. Reduces Net Income but not Cash. Capped when A. Depreciation = 80% of asset value.
EBIT
Earnings Before Interest and Taxes. Also called Operating Income (B7). Revenue minus all operating costs, before financing costs.
Fixed Overhead
$80,000 per quarter non-variable cost covering factory rent, utilities, and non-R&D staff. Cannot be reduced.
The Lag Rule
All decisions submitted in Quarter N take effect at the start of Quarter N+1. Nothing is instantaneous.
Liquidity Freeze
Triggered when Total Debt > 50% of Total Assets, or Cash = $0. Marketing and R&D spending are blocked until solvency is restored.
Logit Demand Model
The mathematical model that converts Attractiveness Scores into market share percentages. Your share = your A / sum of all companies' A.
Net Fixed Assets (A8)
Gross Fixed Assets − Accumulated Depreciation. Directly drives Max Production Capacity (Net Fixed / 100 = max units/quarter).
R&D Maintenance Cost
$15,000/quarter per component level above Level 1. Recurring P&L cost for operating advanced technology.
Retained Earnings (A17)
Cumulative Net Income from all previous quarters. A key component of Total Equity and thus the Weighted Winning Score.
Stockout
When your demand exceeds your inventory. Unmet customer demand spills over to your most-attractive competitor.
Tech Score
Sum of all four component levels (range: 4–12). Determines your product tier and price multiplier ceiling.
Total Equity (A18)
Shareholders' Equity + Retained Earnings. The single most important metric — 40% of your final winning score.
Weighted Score
The final tiebreaker: 0.4 × Equity + 0.4 × Market Share + 0.2 × Brand Equity. Calculated at end of Q8.
